strengths: Deep, current TypeScript/Node.js fullstack expertise with React, REST APIs and AWS (Optilyz, RIDE, re:cinq) — directly covers the core required stack at well above the required level
Already working on LLM/AI-agent infrastructure (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) and has Kubernetes, Docker and CI/CD experience — hits nearly every nice-to-have on the list

Founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map for environmental mapping — demonstrated long-term commitment to sustainability-adjacent products, plus Berlin-based for the on-site requirement
concerns: Substantially overqualified for an entry-level role — nearly two decades of experience and staff/senior titles raise retention and compensation-fit questions
PostgreSQL is not explicitly named; recent database work centers on MongoDB, with SQL/MySQL listed more generically
Nest.js is not mentioned, and German is only basic, which could matter for a German-market energy-consulting platform

key_factors.factor: Core stack mastery (TypeScript, React, REST, relational DBs)
LLM/AI integration and AWS/Kubernetes nice-to-haves already covered
Mission alignment with green/energy domain and Berlin on-site availability

response.justification: The candidate exceeds every required qualification for this role: proven TypeScript fullstack delivery across product companies, hands-on React, REST and cloud infrastructure work, and a testing-first craftsmanship ethos (TDD, pairing, CI/CD) that matches the clean-architecture emphasis. He additionally covers the nice-to-haves most junior applicants lack — LLM/API integration, Kubernetes, AWS — and his founding of an open-source environmental mapping platform signals genuine motivation for the renewable-energy domain rather than incidental interest. Based in Berlin and able to work on-site, he could contribute from day one, mentor as the team grows, and take on responsibility far faster than the role anticipates; the main question is not capability but whether the role's scope satisfies him, which is a matter for a conversation rather than a screen-out.
